assertions.text: Both vehicles (V1 and V2) were traveling northbound on a four-lane, physically divided, bidirectional roadway with two northbound lanes and two southbound lanes.
The roadway segment where the vehicles were traveling had a curve to the right and a slight incline.
V1 was traveling in the rightmost northbound lane (lane one) and V2 was traveling in the adjacent left northbound lane (lane two).
V1 began to move from lane one into lane two while V2 was attempting to pass V1.
V2 departed its lane to the right immediately before impact.
V2's frontal plane impacted V1's rear plane (V2 struck V1 from behind with its front hitting V1's rear).
There was a single impact between V2 and V1 described (one collision where V2's front struck V1's rear).
V2 came to a final rest off the right side of the roadway.
There was a small fire in the engine compartment of V2 after the collision.
After the collision, V1 continued northbound and was not located at the scene.
No braking by either vehicle is mentioned in the narrative (no explicit claim that braking occurred).
No specific speeds are provided for V1 or V2 in the narrative (no stated travel speeds).
The collision severity is not explicitly quantified; described effects include V2 leaving the roadway and a small engine compartment fire.

verdicts.evidence: Telemetry is from V2 only and does not contain information about the heading of V1 or the roadway lane configuration. The available sensor evidence shows V2 speed samples with headings not provided but [speed values between 121.0 km/h and 111.0 km/h](T3); however, there is no GPS heading or lane-count data in the provided channels, so the claim about both vehicles traveling northbound on a four-lane divided roadway is unverifiable.

Road geometry (curve to the right, incline) is not recorded in the vehicle-frame sensors available. Steering shows a change to [30.0 deg at t=0.0](T3) and a peak signed deviation of [35.0 deg](T2), but without map/GPS elevation or known axis alignment these do not prove a rightward curve or incline of the roadway; therefore this assertion is unverifiable.

Lane positions are not present in the telemetry. Steering and speed samples ([steering peaked at 30.0 deg](T3), [speed ranged 121.0–111.0 km/h](T3)) are insufficient to determine that V1 was in lane one and V2 in lane two; assertion is unverifiable.

Sequence of V1 moving into V2's lane while V2 attempted a pass cannot be inferred from the single-vehicle telemetry. Steering shows a rightward steering input to [30.0 deg at t=0.0](T3) and a brake application at [t=-0.5 s](T2), but there is no data about V1's trajectory or relative positions, so this assertion is unverifiable.

The crash-pulse delta-V shows a dominant longitudinal change with a maximum cumulative value of [−81.0 km/h at t=0.16 s](T2) and a much smaller lateral cumulative delta-V of [−11.0 km/h at t=0.09 s](T2), indicating the impact impulse was primarily longitudinal rather than a rightward departure immediately before impact; therefore the claim that V2 departed its lane to the right immediately before impact is contradicted by the telemetry.

EDR labels the event as 'Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)' in the summary and the crash-pulse shows a large negative longitudinal delta-V of [−81.0 km/h at t=0.16 ms](T1) consistent with V2 experiencing a frontal impact into another vehicle's rear; additionally the delta-V stats list the longitudinal maximum as [−81.0 km/h](T2).

Telemetry shows a single dominant crash pulse (longitudinal [−81.0 km/h max](T2)), but single vs multiple contacts (count) cannot be definitively determined from these channels alone; thus the assertion of a single impact is unverifiable.
Final rest position off the right side of the roadway is not recorded in the vehicle telemetry channels provided; there is no GPS position or displacement trace, so this assertion is unverifiable.
Fire in the engine compartment is not captured by the telemetry channels; no thermal or fire-detection channels are present, so presence of fire is unverifiable from sensors.
Whether V1 continued northbound and was not located cannot be determined from V2's telemetry alone; assertion is unverifiable.

Narrative states no braking mentioned; telemetry shows a brake application transition from 0 to 1 at [t=−0.5 s](T2) — therefore the statement that no braking is mentioned in the narrative is a narrative meta-claim and is supported by the fact that telemetry does show a brake event at [t=−0.5 s](T2) which contradicts absence of braking, but the assertion as phrased (that no braking is mentioned) is about the narrative, not the sensors — interpreting it as 'no braking is mentioned' is supported as telemetry indicates braking did occur; specifically, brake channel transitions include a 0→1 at [t=−0.5 s](T2).

Telemetry and the narrative both lack explicit stated travel speeds for V1 or V2 in the narrative; sensor data does record speed samples for V2 (first value [121.0 km/h at t=−5.0 s] and last [111.0 km/h at t=0.0 s]) in T3, but the assertion that no specific speeds are provided in the narrative is supported because no narrative speeds are present.

Severity is not numerically quantified in the narrative; telemetry shows V2 experienced a large longitudinal delta-V of [−81.0 km/h](T2) and an airbag deployment stage at [5.0 ms](T1), and the narrative effects (vehicle leaving roadway and small engine fire) are non-numeric descriptions; thus the assertion that severity is not explicitly quantified is supported.

result.rationale: Telemetry from V2 clearly shows a dominant longitudinal crash pulse ([−81.0 km/h max delta-V](T2)) consistent with a frontal-into-rear impact (EDR event label 'Frontal / Rear Crash')(T1), and a brake application at [t=−0.5 s](T2). However, lane positions, road geometry, fire, final rest location, and claims about V1's motion are not recorded in these channels, making many narrative assertions unverifiable.

citations.claim: speed values between 121.0 km/h and 111.0 km/h

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
